segments.text: The news bloomed across ConSensus a few seconds before Bates called it aloud: Thirteen skimmers had not reappeared from behind Big Ben on schedule. Sixteen. Twenty-eight.

runs.t: The news bloomed across ConSensus a few seconds before Bates called it aloud: Thirteen skimmers had not reappeared from behind Big Ben on schedule. Sixteen. Twenty-eight.

segments.text: And counting.

runs.t: And counting.

segments.text: Sarasti clicked to himself as he and Bates played catch-up. Tactical filled with luminous multicolored threads, a tangle of revised projections as intricate as art. The threads wrapped Ben like a filamentous cocoon; Theseus was a naked speck in the middle distance.

runs.t: Sarasti clicked to himself as he and Bates played catch-up. Tactical filled with luminous multicolored threads, a tangle of revised projections as intricate as art. The threads wrapped Ben like a filamentous cocoon;
Theseus
was a naked speck in the middle distance.

segments.text: I expected any number of those lines to skewer us like needles through a bug. Surprisingly, none did; but the projections only extended twenty-five hours into the future, and were reliable for only half that. Not even Sarasti and the Captain could look so far ahead with that many balls in the air. It was something, though, the faintest silver lining: that all these high-speed behemoths couldn't simply reach out and swat us without warning. Evidently they still had to ease into the curve.

segments.text: After Rorschach's dive, I'd been starting to think the laws of physics didn't apply.

runs.t: After
Rorschach
's dive, I'd been starting to think the laws of physics didn't apply.

segments.text: The trajectories were close enough, though. At least three skimmers would be passing within a hundred kilometers on their next orbits.

runs.t: The trajectories were close enough, though. At least three skimmers would be passing within a hundred kilometers on their next orbits.

segments.text: Sarasti reached for his injector, the blood rising in his face. "Time to go. We refit Charybdis while you're sulking."

runs.t: Sarasti reached for his injector, the blood rising in his face. "Time to go. We refit
Charybdis
while you're sulking."

segments.text: He held the hypo to his throat and shot up. I stared at ConSensus, caught by that bright shifting web like a moth by a streetlight.

runs.t: He held the hypo to his throat and shot up. I stared at ConSensus, caught by that bright shifting web like a moth by a streetlight.

segments.text: "Now, Siri."

runs.t: Now
, Siri."

segments.text: He pushed me from his quarters. I sailed into the passageway, grabbed a convenient rung—and stopped.

runs.t: He pushed me from his quarters. I sailed into the passageway, grabbed a convenient rung—and stopped.

segments.text: The spine was alive with grunts, patrolling the airspace, standing guard over the fab plants and shuttle 'locks, clinging like giant insects to the rungs of unrolling spinal ladders. Slowly, silently, the spine itself was stretching.

runs.t: The spine was alive with grunts, patrolling the airspace, standing guard over the fab plants and shuttle 'locks, clinging like giant insects to the rungs of unrolling spinal ladders. Slowly, silently, the spine itself was
stretching

segments.text: It could do that, I remembered. Its corrugations flexed and relaxed like muscle, it could grow up to two hundred meters to accommodate any late-breaking need for a bigger hanger or more lab space.

runs.t: It could do that, I remembered. Its corrugations flexed and relaxed like muscle, it could grow up to two hundred meters to accommodate any late-breaking need for a bigger hanger or more lab space.

segments.text: Or more infantry. Theseus was increasing the size of the battlefield.

runs.t: Or more infantry.
Theseus
was increasing the size of the battlefield.

segments.text: "Come." The vampire turned aft.

runs.t: "Come." The vampire turned aft.

segments.text: Bates broke in from up front. "Something's happening."

runs.t: Bates broke in from up front. "Something's happening."

segments.text: An emergency handpad, geckoed to the expanding bulkhead, slid past to one side. Sarasti grabbed it and tapped commands. Bates' feed appeared on the bulkhead: a tiny chunk of Big Ben, an EM-enhanced equatorial quadrant only a few thousand klicks on a side. The clouds boiled down there, a cyclonic knot of turbulence swirling almost too fast for realtime. The overlay described charged particles, bound in a deep Parker spiral. It spoke of great mass, rising.

runs.t: An emergency handpad, geckoed to the expanding bulkhead, slid past to one side. Sarasti grabbed it and tapped commands. Bates' feed appeared on the bulkhead: a tiny chunk of Big Ben, an EM-enhanced equatorial quadrant only a few thousand klicks on a side. The clouds boiled down there, a cyclonic knot of turbulence swirling almost too fast for realtime. The overlay described charged particles, bound in a deep Parker spiral. It spoke of great mass, rising.

segments.text: Sarasti clicked.

runs.t: Sarasti clicked.

segments.text: "DTI?" Bates said.

runs.t: "DTI?" Bates said.

segments.text: "Optical only." Sarasti took my arm and dragged me effortlessly astern. The display paced us along the bulkhead: seven skimmers shot from the clouds as I watched, a ragged circle of scramjets screaming red-hot into space. ConSensus plotted their paths in an instant; luminous arcs rose around our ship like the bars of a cage.

runs.t: "Optical only." Sarasti took my arm and dragged me effortlessly astern. segments.text: Theseus shuddered.

runs.t: Theseus
shuddered.

segments.text: We've been hit, I thought. Suddenly the spine's plodding expansion cranked into overdrive; the pleated wall lurched and accelerated, streaming past my outstretched fingers as the closed hatch receded up ahead—

runs.t: We've been hit
, I thought. Suddenly the spine's plodding expansion cranked into overdrive; the pleated wall lurched and accelerated, streaming past my outstretched fingers as the closed hatch receded up ahead—

segments.text: —receded overhead.

runs.t: —receded
overhead

segments.text: The walls weren't moving at all. We were falling, to the sudden strident bleating of an alarm.

runs.t: The walls weren't moving at all. We were
falling
, to the sudden strident bleating of an alarm.

segments.text: Something nearly yanked my arm from its socket: Sarasti had reached out with one hand and caught a rung, reached with his other and caught me before we'd both been flattened against the Fab plant. We dangled. I must have weighed two hundred kilograms; the floor shuddered ten meters below my feet. The ship groaned around us. The spine filled with the screech of torquing metal. Bates' grunts clung to its walls with clawed feet.

runs.t: Something nearly yanked my arm from its socket: Sarasti had reached out with one hand and caught a rung, reached with his other and caught me before we'd both been flattened against the Fab plant. We dangled. I must have weighed two hundred kilograms; the floor shuddered ten meters below my feet. The ship groaned around us. The spine filled with the screech of torquing metal. Bates' grunts clung to its walls with clawed feet.

segments.text: I reached for the ladder. The ladder pulled away: the ship was bending in the middle and down had started to climb the walls. Sarasti and I swung towards the center of the spine like a daisy-chain pendulum.

runs.t: I reached for the ladder. The ladder pulled away: the ship was bending in the middle and
down
had started to climb the walls. Sarasti and I swung towards the center of the spine like a daisy-chain pendulum.

segments.text: "Bates! James!" The vampire roared. His grip on my wrist trembled, slipping. I strained for the ladder, swung, caught it.

runs.t: Bates! James!
" The vampire roared. His grip on my wrist trembled, slipping. I strained for the ladder, swung, caught it.

segments.text: "Susan James has barricaded herself in the bridge and shut down autonomic overrides." An unfamiliar voice, flat and affectless. "She has initiated an unauthorized burn. I have begun a controlled reactor shutdown; be advised that the main drive will be offline for at least twenty-seven minutes."

runs.t: "Susan James has barricaded herself in the bridge and shut down autonomic overrides." An unfamiliar voice, flat and affectless. "She has initiated an unauthorized burn. I have begun a controlled reactor shutdown; be advised that the main drive will be offline for at least twenty-seven minutes."

segments.text: The ship, I realized, its voice raised calmly above the alarm. The Captain itself. On Public Address.

runs.t: The ship
, I realized, its voice raised calmly above the alarm. The Captain itself. On Public Address.

segments.text: That was unusual.

runs.t: That
was unusual.

segments.text: "Bridge!" Sarasti barked. "Open channel!"

runs.t: "Bridge!" Sarasti barked. "Open channel!"

segments.text: Someone was shouting up there. There were words, but I couldn't make them out.

runs.t: Someone was shouting up there. There were words, but I couldn't make them out.

segments.text: Without warning, Sarasti let go.

runs.t: Without warning, Sarasti let go.

segments.text: He dropped obliquely in a blur. Aft and opposite, the bulkhead waited to swat him like an insect. In half a second both his legs would be shattered, if the impact didn't kill him outright—

runs.t: He dropped obliquely in a blur. Aft and opposite, the bulkhead waited to swat him like an insect. In half a second both his legs would be shattered, if the impact didn't kill him outright—

segments.text: But suddenly we were weightless again, and Jukka Sarasti—purple-faced, stiff-limbed— was foaming at the mouth.

runs.t: But suddenly we were weightless again, and Jukka Sarasti—purple-faced, stiff-limbed— was foaming at the mouth.

segments.text: "Reactor offline," the Captain reported. Sarasti bounced off the wall.

runs.t: "Reactor offline," the Captain reported. Sarasti bounced off the wall.

segments.text: He's having a seizure, I realized.

runs.t: He's having a seizure
, I realized.

segments.text: I released the ladder and pushed astern. Theseus swung lopsidedly around me. Sarasti convulsed in mid-air; clicks and hisses and choking sounds stuttered from his mouth. His eyes were so wide they seemed lidless. His pupils were mirror-red pinpoints. The flesh twitched across his face as though trying to crawl off.

segments.text: Ahead and behind, battlebots held their position and ignored us.

segments.text: "Bates!" I yelled up the spine. "We need help!"

!" I yelled up the spine. "We need help!"

segments.text: Angles, everywhere. Seams on the shield plates. Sharp shadows and protrusions on the surface of every drone. A two-by-three matrix of insets, bordered in black, floating over the main ConSensus display: two big interlinked crosses right in front of where Sarasti had been hanging.

segments.text: This can't be happening. He just took his antiEuclideans. I saw him. Unless...

segments.text: Someone had spiked Sarasti's drugs.

segments.text: "Bates!" She should be linked into the grunts, they should have leapt forward at the first sign of trouble. They should be dragging my commander to the infirmary by now. They waited stolid and immobile. I stared at the nearest: "Bates, you there?" And then—in case she wasn't—I spoke to the grunt directly. "Are you autonomous? Do you take verbal orders?"

segments.text: I pushed. Sarasti's arms flailed randomly against my head and shoulders. He tumbled forward and sideways, hit the moving ConSensus display dead center, bounced away up the spine. I kicked off in his wake—

segments.text: —and glimpsed something from the corner of my eye—

segments.text: —and turned—

runs.t: —and turned—

segments.text: —And dead center of ConSensus, Rorschach erupted from Ben's seething face like a breaching whale. It wasn't just the EM-enhance: the thing was glowing, deep angry red. Enraged, it hurled itself into space, big as a mountain range.

segments.text: Fuck fuck fuck.

segments.text: Theseus lurched. The lights flickered, went out, came back on again. The turning bulkhead cuffed me from behind.

runs.t: Theseus
lurched. The lights flickered, went out, came back on again. The turning bulkhead cuffed me from behind.

segments.text: "Backups engaged," the Captain said calmly.

runs.t: "Backups engaged," the Captain said calmly.

segments.text: "Captain! Sarasti's down!" I kicked off the nearest ladder, bumped into a grunt and headed forward after the vampire. "Bates isn't—what do I do?"

runs.t: "Captain! Sarasti's down!" I kicked off the nearest ladder, bumped into a grunt and headed forward after the vampire. "Bates isn't—what do I do?"

segments.text: "Nav offline. Starboard afferents offline."

runs.t: "Nav offline. Starboard afferents offline."

segments.text: It wasn't even talking to me, I realized. Maybe this wasn't the Captain at all. Maybe it was pure reflex: a dialog tree, spouting public-service announcements. Maybe Theseus had already been lobotomized. Maybe this was only her brain stem talking.

Theseus
had already been lobotomized. Maybe this was only her brain stem talking.

segments.text: Darkness again. Then flickering light.

runs.t: Darkness again. Then flickering light.

segments.text: If the Captain was gone, we were screwed.

runs.t: If the Captain was gone, we were screwed.

segments.text: I gave Sarasti another push. The alarm bleated on. The drum was twenty meters ahead; BioMed was just the other side of that closed hatch. The hatch had been open before, I remembered. Someone had shut it in the last few minutes. Fortunately Theseus had no locks on her doors.

segments.text: Who in hell…?

segments.text: Ten meters to the drum. Theseus jerked again, slowed her spin. Stabilised.

Theseus

segments.text: "Somebody start the goddamned reactor! I've only got attitude jets up here!"

runs.t: Somebody start the goddamned reactor
! I've only got attitude jets up here!"

segments.text: "Susan? Sascha?" I was at the hatch. "Who is that?" I pushed passed Sarasti and reached to open it.

that?" I pushed passed Sarasti and reached to open it.

segments.text: No answer.

runs.t: No answer.

segments.text: Not from ConSensus, anyway. I heard a muted hum from behind, saw the ominous shifting of shadows on the bulkhead just a moment too late. I turned in time to see one of the grunts raise a spiky appendage—curved like a scimitar, needle-tipped—over Sarasti's head.

runs.t: Not from ConSensus, anyway. I heard a muted

from behind, saw the ominous shifting of shadows on the bulkhead just a moment too late. I turned in time to see one of the grunts raise a spiky appendage—curved like a scimitar, needle-tipped—over Sarasti's head.

segments.text: I turned in time to see it plunge into his skull.

runs.t: I turned in time to see it plunge into his skull.

segments.text: I froze. The metal proboscis withdrew, dark and slick. Lateral maxillipeds began nibbling at the base of Sarasti's skull. His pithed corpse wasn't thrashing now; it only trembled, a sack of muscles and motor nerves awash in static.

segments.text: Bates.

runs.t: Bates

segments.text: Her mutiny was underway. No, their mutiny—Bates and the Gang. I'd known. I'd imagined it. I'd seen it coming.

runs.t: Her mutiny was underway. No,
their
mutiny—Bates and the Gang. I'd known. I'd imagined it. I'd seen it coming.

segments.text: He hadn't believed me.

runs.t: He hadn't believed me.

segments.text: The lights went out again. The alarm fell silent. ConSensus dwindled to a flickering doodle on the bulkhead and disappeared; I saw something there in that last instant, and refused to process it. I heard breath catch in my throat, felt angular monstrosities advancing through the darkness. Something flared directly ahead, a bright brief staccato in the void. I glimpsed curves and angles in silhouette, staggering. The buzzing crackle of shorting circuitry. Metal objects collided nearby, unseen.

I glimpsed curves and angles in silhouette,
staggering
. The buzzing crackle of shorting circuitry. Metal objects collided nearby, unseen.

segments.text: From behind the crinkle of the drum hatch, opening. A sudden beam of harsh chemical light hit me as I turned, lit the mechanical ranks behind; they simultaneously unclamped from their anchorages and floated free. Their joints clicked in unison like an army stamping to attention

segments.text: "Keeton!" Bates snapped, sailing through the hatch. "You okay?"

runs.t: Keeton!
" Bates snapped, sailing through the hatch. "You okay?"

segments.text: The chemlight shone from her forehead. It turned the interior of the spine into a high-contrast mosaic, all pale surfaces and sharp moving shadows. It spilled across the grunt that had killed Sarasti; the robot bounced down the spine, suddenly, mysteriously inert. The light washed across Sarasti's body. The corpse turned slowly on its axis. Spherical crimson beads emerged from its head like drops of water from a leaky faucet. They spread in a winding, widening trail, spot-lit by Bates' headlamp: a spiral arm of dark ruby suns.

Spherical crimson beads emerged from its head like drops of water from a leaky faucet. They spread in a winding, widening trail, spot-lit by Bates' headlamp: a spiral arm of dark ruby suns.

segments.text: I backed away. "You—"

runs.t: I backed away. "You—"

segments.text: She pushed me to one side. "Stay clear of the hatch, unless you're going through." Her eyes were fixed on the ranked drones. "Optical line of sight."

runs.t: She pushed me to one side. "Stay clear of the hatch, unless you're going through." Her eyes were fixed on the ranked drones. "Optical line of sight."

segments.text: Rows of glassy eyes reflected back at us down the passageway, passing in and out of shadow.

runs.t: Rows of glassy eyes reflected back at us down the passageway, passing in and out of shadow.

segments.text: "You killed Sarasti!"

runs.t: "You killed Sarasti!"

segments.text: "No."

runs.t: "No."

segments.text: "But—"

runs.t: "But—"

segments.text: "Who do you think shut it down, Keeton? The fucker went rogue. I could barely even get it to self-destruct." Her eyes went briefly deep-focus; all down the spine the surviving drones launched into some intricate martial ballet, half-seen in the shifting cone of her headlamp.

range? I thought we were—the burn—"

segments.text: "Sent us in the wrong direction. We're inbound."

runs.t: "Sent us in the wrong direction. We're inbound."

segments.text: "She fucked up."

segments.text: "Not by that much. She couldn't have." We were all rated for manual piloting. Just in case.

runs.t: "Not the Gang," Bates said.

segments.text: "But—"

runs.t: "But—"

segments.text: "I think there's someone new in there now. Bunch of submodules wired together and woke up somehow, I don't know. But whatever's in charge, I think it's just panicking."

segments.text: Stuttering brightness on all sides. The spinal lightstrips flickered and finally held steady, at half their usual brightness.

segments.text: Theseus coughed static and spoke: "ConSensus is offline. Reac—"

coughed static and spoke: "ConSensus is offline. Reac—"

segments.text: ConSensus, I remembered as Bates turned to head back upstream.

segments.text: "I saw something," I said. "Before ConSensus went out."

segments.text: "Yeah."

runs.t: "Yeah."

segments.text: "Was that—"

runs.t: "Was that—"

segments.text: She paused at the hatch. "Yeah."

runs.t: She paused at the hatch. "Yeah."

segments.text: I'd seen scramblers. Hundreds of them, sailing naked through the void, their arms spread wide.

runs.t: I'd seen scramblers. Hundreds of them, sailing naked through the void, their arms spread wide.

segments.text: Some of their arms, anyway. "They were carrying—"

runs.t: Some of their arms, anyway. "They were carrying—"

segments.text: Bates nodded. "Weapons." Her eyes flickered to some unseen distance for a moment. "First wave headed for the front end. Blister and forward lock, I think. Second wave's aft." She shook her head. "Huh. I would have done it the other way around."

segments.text: "Far?" Bates smiled faintly. "They're already on the hull, Siri. We're engaging."

segments.text: "What do I do? What do I do?"

segments.text: "No."

runs.t: "No."

segments.text: "Why not? They can fight better without you, you said that yourself! What's the point?"

runs.t: "Why not? They can fight better
without
you, you said that yourself! What's the
point

segments.text: "Can't leave yourself a back door, Keeton. Defeats the whole purpose." She allowed herself a small, sad smile. "They've breached. Go."

runs.t: "Can't leave yourself a back door, Keeton. Defeats the whole purpose." She allowed herself a small, sad smile. "They've breached. Go."

segments.text: She was gone, fresh alarms rising in her wake. Far towards the bow I heard the crinkle of emergency bulkheads snapping shut.

runs.t: She was gone, fresh alarms rising in her wake. Far towards the bow I heard the crinkle of emergency bulkheads snapping shut.

segments.text: Sarasti's undead carcass gurgled and pushed me down the spine. Four more grunts slid smoothly past and took up position behind us. I looked over my shoulder in time to see the vampire pull the handpad from the wall. But it wasn't Sarasti at all, of course. It was the Captain—whatever was left of the Captain, this far into the fight—commandeering a peripheral interface for its own use. The optical port sprouted conspicuously from the back of Sarasti's neck, where the cable used to go in; I remembered the drone's maxillipeds, chewing.

runs.t: Sarasti's undead carcass gurgled and pushed me down the spine. Four more grunts slid smoothly past and took up position behind us. I looked over my shoulder in time to see the vampire pull the handpad from the wall. But it wasn't Sarasti at all, of course. It was the Captain—whatever was left of the Captain, this far into the fight—commandeering a peripheral interface for its own use. The optical port sprouted conspicuously from the back of Sarasti's neck, where the cable used to go in; I remembered the drone's maxillipeds, chewing.

segments.text: The sound of weapons fire and ricochets rose behind us.

runs.t: The sound of weapons fire and ricochets rose behind us.

segments.text: The corpse typed one-handed as we moved. I wondered briefly why it just didn't talk before my gaze flickered back to the spike in his brain: Sarasti's speech centers must be mush.

runs.t: The corpse typed one-handed as we moved. I wondered briefly why it just didn't
talk
before my gaze flickered back to the spike in his brain: Sarasti's speech centers must be mush.

segments.text: "Why did you kill him?" I said. A whole new alarm started up, way back in the drum. A sudden breeze tugged me backward for a moment, dissipated in the next second with a distant clang.

runs.t: "Why did you kill him?" I said. A whole new alarm started up, way back in the drum. A sudden
breeze
tugged me backward for a moment, dissipated in the next second with a distant clang.

segments.text: The corpse held out the handpad, configured for keys and a text display: Seizng. Cldnt cntrl.

runs.t: The corpse held out the handpad, configured for keys and a text display: Seizng. Cldnt cntrl.

segments.text: We were at the shuttle locks. Robot soldiers let us pass, their attention elsewhere.

runs.t: We were at the shuttle locks. Robot soldiers let us pass, their attention elsewhere.

segments.text: U go, the Captain said.

runs.t: U go, the Captain said.

segments.text: Someone screamed in the distance. Way off up the spine, the drum hatch slammed shut; I turned and saw a pair of distant grunts welding the seal. They seemed to move faster now than they ever had before. Maybe it was only my imagination.

runs.t: Someone screamed in the distance. Way off up the spine, the drum hatch slammed shut; I turned and saw a pair of distant grunts welding the seal. They seemed to move faster now than they ever had before. Maybe it was only my imagination.

segments.text: The starboard shuttle lock slid back. Charybdis' interior lights winked on, spilling brightness into the passageway; the spine's emergency lighting seemed even dimmer in contrast. I peered through the opening. There was almost no cabin space left—just a single open coffin jammed between coolant and fuel tanks and massive retrofitted shockpads. Charybdis had been refitted for high-G and long distance.

runs.t: The starboard shuttle lock slid back.
Charybdis

' interior lights winked on, spilling brightness into the passageway; the spine's emergency lighting seemed even dimmer in contrast. I peered through the opening. There was almost no cabin space left—just a single open coffin jammed between coolant and fuel tanks and massive retrofitted shockpads.
Charybdis
had been refitted for high-G and long distance.

segments.text: And me.

runs.t: And me.

segments.text: Sarasti's corpse urged me on from behind. I turned and faced it.

runs.t: Sarasti's corpse urged me on from behind. I turned and faced it.

segments.text: "Was it ever him?" I asked.

runs.t: "Was it
ever
him?" I asked.

segments.text: Go.

runs.t: Go.

segments.text: "Tell me. Did he ever speak for himself? Did he decide anything on his own? Were we ever following his orders, or was it just you all along?"

runs.t: Tell me
. Did he ever speak for himself? Did he decide
anything
on his own? Were we ever following
his
orders, or was it just you all along?"

segments.text: Sarasti's undead eyes stared glassy and uncomprehending. His fingers jerked on the handpad.

runs.t: Sarasti's undead eyes stared glassy and uncomprehending. His fingers jerked on the handpad.

segments.text: U dislke ordrs frm mchnes. Happier ths way.

runs.t: U dislke ordrs frm mchnes. Happier ths way.

segments.text: I let it strap me in and close the lid. I lay there in the dark, feeling my body lurch and sway as the shuttle slid into its launch slot. I withstood the sudden silence as the docking clamps let go, the jerk of acceleration that spat me hard into the vacuum, the ongoing thrust that pushed against my chest like a soft mountain. Around me the shuttle trembled in the throes of a burn that far exceeded its normative specs.

runs.t: I let it strap me in and close the lid. I lay there in the dark, feeling my body lurch and sway as the shuttle slid into its launch slot. I withstood the sudden silence as the docking clamps let go, the jerk of acceleration that spat me hard into the vacuum, the ongoing thrust that pushed against my chest like a soft mountain. Around me the shuttle trembled in the throes of a burn that far exceeded its normative specs.

segments.text: My inlays came back online. Suddenly I could see outside if I wanted. I could see what was happening behind me.

runs.t: My inlays came back online. Suddenly I could see
outside
if I wanted. I could see what was happening behind me.

segments.text: I chose not to, deliberately and fervently, and looked anyway.

runs.t: I chose not to, deliberately and fervently, and looked anyway.

segments.text: Theseus was dwindling by then, even on tactical. She listed down the well, wobbling toward some enemy rendezvous that must have been intentional, some last-second maneuver to get her payload as close to target as possible. Rorschach rose to meet her, its gnarled spiky arms uncoiling, spreading as if in anticipation of an embrace. But it was the backdrop, not the players, that stole the tableau: the face of Big Ben roiling in my rearview, a seething cyclonic backdrop filling the window. Magnetic contours wound spring-tight on the overlay; Rorschach was drawing all of Ben's magnetosphere around itself like a bright swirling cloak, twisting it into a concentrated knot that grew and brightened and bulged outward...

runs.t: Theseus

was dwindling by then, even on tactical. She listed down the well, wobbling toward some enemy rendezvous that must have been intentional, some last-second maneuver to get her payload as close to target as possible.
Rorschach
rose to meet her, its gnarled spiky arms
uncoiling

, spreading as if in anticipation of an embrace. But it was the backdrop, not the players, that stole the tableau: the face of Big Ben roiling in my rearview, a seething cyclonic backdrop filling the window. Magnetic contours wound spring-tight on the overlay;
Rorschach
was drawing all of Ben's magnetosphere around itself like a bright swirling cloak, twisting it into a concentrated knot that grew and brightened and bulged outward...

segments.text: Like a torsion flare from an L-class dwarf, my commander had said once, but we should see anything big enough to generate that effect and the sky's dark on that bearing. IAU calls it a statistical artefact.

runs.t: Like a torsion flare from an L-class dwarf,
my commander had said once,
but we should see anything big enough to generate that effect and the sky's dark on that bearing. IAU calls it a statistical artefact.

segments.text: As, in fact, it had been. An impact splash perhaps, or the bright brief bellow of some great energy source rebooting after a million years of dormancy. Much like this one: a solar flare, with no sun beneath it. A magnetic cannon ten thousand times stronger than nature gave it any right to be.

runs.t: As, in fact, it had been. An impact splash perhaps, or the bright brief bellow of some great energy source rebooting after a million years of dormancy. Much like this one: a solar flare, with no sun beneath it. A magnetic cannon ten thousand times stronger than nature gave it any right to be.

segments.text: Both sides drew their weapons. I don't know which fired first, or even if it mattered: how many tonnes of antimatter would it take to match something that could squeeze the power of a sun from a gas ball barely wider than Jupiter? Was Rorschach also resigned to defeat, had each side opted for a kamikaze strike on the other?

runs.t: Both sides drew their weapons. I don't know which fired first, or even if it mattered: how many tonnes of antimatter would it take to match something that could squeeze the power of a sun from a gas ball barely wider than Jupiter? Was
Rorschach
also resigned to defeat, had each side opted for a kamikaze strike on the other?

segments.text: I don't know. Big Ben got in the way just minutes before the explosion. That's probably why I'm still alive. Ben stood between me and that burning light like a coin held against the sun.

runs.t: I don't know. Big Ben got in the way just minutes before the explosion. That's probably why I'm still alive. Ben stood between me and that burning light like a coin held against the sun.

segments.text: Theseus sent everything it could, until the last microsecond. Every recorded moment of hand-to-hand combat, every last countdown, every last soul. All the moves and all the vectors. I have that telemetry. I can break it down into any number of shapes, continuous or discrete. I can transform the topology, rotate it and compress it and serve it up in dialects that any ally might be able to use. Perhaps Sarasti was right, perhaps some of it is vital.

runs.t: Theseus

sent everything it could, until the last microsecond. Every recorded moment of hand-to-hand combat, every last countdown, every last soul. All the moves and all the vectors. I have that telemetry. I can break it down into any number of shapes, continuous or discrete. I can transform the topology, rotate it and compress it and serve it up in dialects that any ally might be able to use. Perhaps Sarasti was right, perhaps some of it is vital.

segments.text: I don't know what any of it means.

runs.t: I don't know what any of it means.
